title: Exposing an encroacher's paradise
desc: A secluded strip of sand cradled by forested hills, Freedom Beach in Phuket has long been prized for its natural beauty -- clear waters, dense greenery and a tranquillity cherished by local communities who lived and worked in harmony with the land.

title: High hopes for cancer control
desc: The Ministry of Public Health is moving to draft Thailand's first Cancer Control Act, raising hopes for improved access to treatment, stronger prevention and greater equity in medical resources as the country faces a growing cancer burden and mounting healthcare costs.

title: PP rejects ethics breach over reforms
desc: The People's Party (PP) insists it has a right to propose laws, even if they turn out to be unpopular.

title: Governors can declare disaster sites
desc: Provincial governors have the authority to declare their provinces disaster zones amid rising levels of air pollution, says Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul.

title: PM axes Phiphat as his war centre head
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul will appoint Ekniti Nitithanprapas to head the Middle East war monitoring centre, replacing Phiphat Ratchakitprakarn, in a move aimed at easing public concern.

title: Commercial EVs picked to propel Thai boom
desc: Thailand's battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is expected to gain significant momentum this year, with sales projected to surpass 120,000 units partially due to soaring oil prices, says the Electric Vehicle Association of Thailand (EVAT).

title: Fairer oil pricing vowed
desc: Energy Minister Akanat Promphan has pledged to overhaul the country's refinery margin mechanism to ensure fairer pricing for consumers.

title: SEC bans auditor after Stark scandal
desc: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has intensified enforcement in the high-profile accounting scandal of Stark Corp, filing a criminal complaint and imposing a six-year ban on an auditor from Deloitte for serious professional misconduct.

title: Polluted Chiang Mai to suffer at Songkran
desc: Northern tourism operators expect a poor showing during the Songkran holiday as the region remains choked by smog, amid weak spending attributed to the war in the Middle East and the subsequent oil shock.

title: Panel slashes GDP growth forecast
desc: The Joint Standing Committee on Commerce, Industry and Banking (JSCCIB) has lowered its Thai GDP growth forecast to a range of 1.2-1.6%, citing the ongoing energy crisis triggered by war in the Middle East. The previous projection was 1.6-2.0%.

title: Thai diesel prices rise another 3.50 baht
desc: Diesel prices will rise another 3.50 baht a litre to 44.24 baht — 48% higher than a month ago — at service stations across Thailand on Thursday morning.

title: Banks raise reserves to deal with effects of war
desc: Thai banks are increasing their loan-loss reserves to prepare for the impact of a prolonged war in the Middle East and to support a growing number of vulnerable customers.

title: Power bills to rise only 1.8%
desc: Electricity tariffs in Thailand will rise by only 1.8% for the May-August period, the Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) announced on Wednesday.

title: Fuel crisis powers surge in EV interest in Asia
desc: Motorists across the Asia-Pacific region are switching to electric vehicles at a rapid pace, as rising fuel costs due to the Middle East war force consumers and companies ​to reconsider their reliance on petrol and diesel vehicles.

title: Thailand Post the latest to add fuel surcharge
desc: Thailand Post plans to impose a fuel surcharge of 3 baht per item on domestic Express Mail Service (EMS) and e-CoPost services from April 16, citing higher fuel costs.

title: Exhausted firefighter dies on Chiang Mai mountain
desc: CHIANG MAI - A local volunteer battling wildfires in this northern province was found dead on a mountain in Mae Rim district on Wednesday after going missing from his group the night before.

title: Thai business group cuts 2026 GDP growth forecast to 1.2%-1.6%
desc: Thailand's economy is expected to ​grow by 1.2% to 1.6% this year, a leading business group said on Wednesday, cutting its outlook from ​an

title: The Bangkok Motor Show, after the fuel shock
desc: The Bangkok International Motor Show still knows how to stage desire. This year’s edition, running from March 25 to April 5 at IMPACT Challenger, has all the familiar pleasures intact: polished bodywork under hard lights, crowds drifting from stand to stand, and the quiet thrill of being close to machines designed to look smoother, sharper and more complete than everyday life usually...

title: 泰总理：部长不是来“试工”的，干不好就换人
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月1日上午，泰国总理兼内政部长、泰自豪党党魁阿努廷就新一届内阁（被称为“阿努廷2.0内阁”）的组建情况接受媒体采访。他明确表示，部长职位不是用来试工的地方。接下来，他将对每位部长的工作进行绩效考核（KPI），如果谁干得不好、达不到目标，他有权随时调整人事，确保政府高效运转。

title: Eurovision Song Contest makes its Asian debut
desc: This is not an April Fool's Day joke. Bangkok has been announced as the host city of the inaugural Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026.

title: 曼谷一汽车冲进便利店，造成8人受伤
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月31日约22点50分，泰国曼谷Chanasongkram警察局接到报警称，Phra Nakhon区Wat Bowonniwet Vihara寺旁的Bang Lamphu环岛附近，有一辆汽车冲进了便利店里。造成现场8人受伤，包括2名德国人和6名泰国人。

title: 他信被列入10名符合假释囚犯名单
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月31日，泰国司法部的消息人士透露，曼谷Klong Prem中央监狱假释审查委员会于今天召开会议，审查符合假释条件的囚犯名单，其中包括前总理他信。

title: 曼谷！卡地亚女性创业家奖首次落户东南亚
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月1日，“卡地亚女性创业家奖”正式揭晓了今年的30名获奖者名单，同时宣布将于6月10日在曼谷举办全球颁奖典礼，这是该活动首次在东南亚举办。

title: 中国男子声称被同胞追杀，好心泰国女子出手相助
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月1日，泰国暖武里府Bang Yai警察局透露，3月30日凌晨，一名泰国女子偶遇一名惊慌失措的中国男子向其求助，该男子自称被中国人的诈骗团伙从柬埔寨骗来泰国并遭到追杀。
